text: dhritarashtra uvaca dharma-kshetre kuru-kshetre samaveta yuyutsavah mamakah pandavascaiva kim akurvata sanjaya
translation: Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjaya, gathered on the holy field of Kurukshetra, desiring to fight, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do?
keywords: Kurukshetra, battle, Dhritarashtra, Sanjaya

text: evam uktvarjunah sankhye rathopastha upavisat visrjya sa-saram capam soka-samvigna-manasah
translation: Having spoken thus on the battlefield, Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows, and sat down on the chariot, his mind overwhelmed with grief.
keywords: grief, dejection, Arjuna, bow, battlefield

text: klaibyam ma sma gamah partha naitat tvayy upapadyate kshudram hrdaya-daurbalyam tyaktvottishtha parantapa
translation: O son of Pritha, do not yield to this degrading impotence. It does not become you. Give up such petty weakness of heart and arise, O chastiser of the enemy.
keywords: weakness, arise, courage, Arjuna, strength, Partha

transliterations.ml: ക്ലൈബ്യം മാ സ്മ ഗമഃ പാർഥ നൈതത്ത്വയ്യുപപദ്യതേ
transliterations.hi: क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते
transliterations.ta: க்லைப்யம் மா ஸ்ம கம: பார்த்த நைதத்த்வய்யுபபத்யதே
transliterations.te: క్లైబ్యం మా స్మ గమః పార్థ నైతత్త్వయ్యుపపద్యతే
transliterations.kn: ಕ್ಲೈಬ್ಯಂ ಮಾ ಸ್ಮ ಗಮಃ ಪಾರ್ಥ ನೈತತ್ತ್ವಯ್ಯುಪಪದ್ಯತೇ

text: karpanya-dosopahata-svabhavah prcchami tvam dharma-sammudha-cetah yac chreyah syan niscitam bruhi tan me sisyas te 'ham sadhi mam tvam prapannam
translation: My heart is overpowered by the taint of pity, my mind is confused about duty. I ask You to tell me what is best for me. I am Your disciple. Instruct me, who has taken refuge in You.
keywords: duty, confusion, disciple, refuge, guidance

text: sri-bhagavan uvaca asocyan anvasocas tvam prajna-vadams ca bhasase gatasun agatasums ca nanusocanti panditah
translation: The Supreme Lord said: While speaking learned words, you are mourning for what is not worthy of grief. Those who are wise lament neither for the living nor for the dead.
keywords: wisdom, grief, soul, death, knowledge

text: dehino 'smin yatha dehe kaumaram yauvanam jara tatha dehantara-praptir dhiras tatra na muhyati
translation: As the embodied soul continuously passes from childhood to youth to old age, the soul similarly passes into another body at death. A self-realized soul is not bewildered by such a change.
keywords: soul, reincarnation, body, death, self-realization

text: matra-sparsas tu kaunteya sitosna-sukha-duhkha-dah agamapayino 'nityas tams titiksasva bharata
translation: O son of Kunti, the contact between the senses and their objects gives rise to cold, heat, pleasure, and pain. They are transient, arising and disappearing. Bear them patiently, O descendant of Bharata.
keywords: senses, pleasure, pain, patience, transient

text: avinasi tu tad viddhi yena sarvam idam tatam vinasam avyayasyasya na kascit kartum arhati
translation: That which pervades the entire body you should know to be indestructible. No one can destroy the imperishable soul.
keywords: soul, indestructible, imperishable, eternal

text: ya enam vetti hantaram yas cainam manyate hatam ubhau tau na vijanito nayam hanti na hanyate
translation: Neither he who thinks the living entity the slayer nor he who thinks it slain is in knowledge, for the self slays not nor is slain.
keywords: self, slayer, slain, knowledge, soul, immortal

transliterations.ml: യ ഏനം വേത്തി ഹന്താരം യശ്ചൈനം മന്യതേ ഹതം
transliterations.hi: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्
transliterations.ta: ய ஏநம் வேத்தி ஹந்தாரம் யஶ்சைநம் மந்யதே ஹதம்
transliterations.te: య ఏనం వేత్తి హన్తారం యశ్చైనం మన్యతే హతమ్
transliterations.kn: ಯ ಏನಂ ವೇತ್ತಿ ಹನ್ತಾರಂ ಯಶ್ಚೈನಂ ಮನ್ಯತೇ ಹತಮ್

text: na jayate mriyate va kadacin nayam bhutva bhavita va na bhuyah ajo nityah sasvato 'yam purano na hanyate hanyamane sarire
translation: The soul is never born, nor does it die. It has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. It is unborn, eternal, ever-existing, and primeval. It is not slain when the body is slain.
keywords: soul, eternal, unborn, immortal, death

text: vasamsi jirnani yatha vihaya navani grhnati naro 'parani tatha sarirani vihaya jirnany anyani samyati navani dehi
translation: As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.
keywords: body, soul, garments, reincarnation, change

text: jatasya hi dhruvo mrtyur dhruvam janma mrtasya ca tasmad apariharye rthe na tvam socitum arhasi
translation: For one who has taken birth, death is certain; and for one who has died, birth is certain. Therefore, in the unavoidable discharge of your duty, you should not grieve.
keywords: birth, death, certain, duty, grief, cycle

transliterations.hi: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च
transliterations.ta: ஜாதஸ்ய ஹி த்ருவோ ம்ருத்யுர் த்ருவம் ஜன்ம ம்ருதஸ்ய ச
transliterations.te: జాతస్య హి ధ్రువో మృత్యుర్ధ్రువం జన్మ మృతస్య చ
transliterations.kn: ಜಾತಸ್ಯ ಹಿ ಧ್ರುವೋ ಮೃತ್ಯುರ್ಧ್ರುವಂ ಜನ್ಮ ಮೃತಸ್ಯ ಚ

text: karmany evadhikaras te ma phalesu kadacana ma karma-phala-hetur bhur ma te sango 'stv akarmani
translation: You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to not doing your duty.
keywords: duty, karma, action, fruits, detachment, nishkama karma

text: yoga-sthah kuru karmani sangam tyaktva dhananjaya siddhy-asiddhyoh samo bhutva samatvam yoga ucyate
translation: Perform your duty equipoised, O Arjuna, abandoning all attachment to success or failure. Such equanimity is called yoga.
keywords: yoga, equanimity, duty, detachment, balance

text: prajahati yada kaman sarvan partha mano-gatan atmany evatmana tushtah sthita-prajnas tadocyate
translation: When a man completely casts off all the desires of the mind and is satisfied in the Self by the Self, then he is said to be one of steady wisdom.
keywords: desire, mind, self, satisfied, steady wisdom, sthitaprajna

transliterations.hi: प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान्
transliterations.ta: ப்ரஜஹாதி யதா காமான் ஸர்வான் பார்த்த மநோகதான்

text: dhyayato visayan pumsah sangas tesupajayate sangat sanjayate kamah kamat krodho 'bhijayate
translation: While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.
keywords: desire, attachment, anger, senses, lust, contemplation

text: krodhad bhavati sammohah sammohat smrti-vibhramah smrti-bhramsad buddhi-naso buddhi-nasat pranasyati
translation: From anger comes delusion, from delusion bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost, and when intelligence is lost one falls down again into the material pool.
keywords: anger, delusion, memory, intelligence, downfall

text: yajnarthat karmano 'nyatra loko 'yam karma-bandhanah tad-artham karma kaunteya mukta-sangah samacara
translation: Work done as a sacrifice for Vishnu has to be performed; otherwise work causes bondage in this material world. Therefore, O son of Kunti, perform your prescribed duties for His satisfaction, and in that way you will always remain free from bondage.
keywords: sacrifice, duty, bondage, freedom, karma yoga

text: tasmad asaktah satatam karyam karma samacara asakto hy acaran karma param apnoti purusah
translation: Therefore, without being attached to the fruits of activities, one should act as a matter of duty, for by working without attachment one attains the Supreme.
keywords: detachment, duty, action, Supreme, liberation

text: yad yad acarati sresthas tat tad evetaro janah sa yat pramanam kurute lokas tad anuvartate
translation: Whatever action a great man performs, common men follow. And whatever standards he sets by exemplary acts, all the world pursues.
keywords: leadership, example, standards, influence, great man

text: prakrteh kriyamanani gunaih karmani sarvasah ahankara-vimudhatma kartaham iti manyate
translation: The spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature.
keywords: ego, false ego, nature, modes, doership, prakruti

text: sreyan sva-dharmo vigunah para-dharmat svanushthitat sva-dharme nidhanam sreyah para-dharmo bhayavahah
translation: It is far better to discharge one's prescribed duties, even though faultily, than another's duties perfectly. Destruction in the course of performing one's own duty is better than engaging in another's duties, for to follow another's path is dangerous.
keywords: svadharma, own duty, another's duty, dangerous, death, better

text: yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata abhyutthanam adharmasya tadatmanam srjamy aham
translation: Whenever and wherever there is a decline in righteousness and a predominant rise of unrighteousness, at that time I manifest Myself.
keywords: dharma, righteousness, avatar, incarnation, divine manifestation

text: paritranaya sadhunam vinasaya ca duskrtam dharma-samsthapanarthaya sambhavami yuge yuge
translation: To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I advent Myself millennium after millennium.
keywords: protection, pious, evil, dharma, avatar, yuga

text: karmany akarma yah pasyed akarmani ca karma yah sa buddhiman manushyeshu sa yuktah krtsna-karma-krt
translation: One who sees inaction in action, and action in inaction, is intelligent among men, and he is in the transcendental position, although engaged in all sorts of activities.
keywords: action, inaction, intelligence, transcendental, wisdom, karma

text: sreyan dravya-mayad yajnaj jnana-yajnah parantapa sarvam karmakhilam partha jnane parisamapyate
translation: O chastiser of the enemy, the sacrifice performed in knowledge is better than the mere sacrifice of material possessions. After all, O son of Pritha, all sacrifices of work culminate in transcendental knowledge.
keywords: knowledge, sacrifice, wisdom, transcendence

text: tad viddhi pranipatena pariprasnena sevaya upadeksyanti te jnanam jnaninas tattva-darsinah
translation: Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.
keywords: guru, master, knowledge, truth, inquiry, service, submission

text: brahmany adhaya karmani sangam tyaktva karoti yah lipyate na sa papena padma-patram ivambhasa
translation: One who performs his duty without attachment, surrendering the results to the Supreme God, is unaffected by sinful action, as the lotus leaf is untouched by water.
keywords: lotus, detachment, sin, purity, surrender, duty

text: vidya-vinaya-sampanne brahmane gavi hastini suni caiva sva-pake ca panditah sama-darsinah
translation: The humble sages, by virtue of true knowledge, see with equal vision a learned and gentle brahmana, a cow, an elephant, a dog and a dog-eater.
keywords: equality, vision, wisdom, humility, brahman

text: ye hi samsparsa-ja bhoga duhkha-yonaya eva te ady-antavantah kaunteya na teshu ramate budhah
translation: An intelligent person does not take part in the sources of misery, which are due to contact with the material senses. O son of Kunti, such pleasures have a beginning and an end, and so the wise man does not delight in them.
keywords: pleasure, misery, senses, beginning, end, wise, contact

text: uddhared atmanatmanam natmanam avasadayet atmaiva hy atmano bandhur atmaiva ripur atmanah
translation: One must elevate, not degrade, oneself by one's own mind. The mind is the friend of the conditioned soul, and his enemy as well.
keywords: self, mind, friend, enemy, elevation, meditation

text: bandhur atmatmanas tasya yenatmaivatmana jitah anatmanas tu satrutve vartetatmaiva satru-vat
translation: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends; but for one who has failed to do so, his very mind will be the greatest enemy.
keywords: mind, control, friend, enemy, conquest, self-mastery

text: cancalam hi manah krsna pramathi balavad drdham tasyaham nigraham manye vayor iva su-duskaram
translation: The mind is restless, turbulent, obstinate and very strong, O Krishna, and to subdue it, I think, is more difficult than controlling the wind.
keywords: mind, restless, control, difficult, wind, meditation

text: asamsayam maha-baho mano durnigraham calam abhyasena tu kaunteya vairagyena ca grhyate
translation: O mighty-armed son of Kunti, it is undoubtedly very difficult to curb the restless mind, but it is possible by suitable practice and by detachment.
keywords: mind, restless, practice, detachment, possible, difficult, abhyasa, vairagya

text: mattah parataram nanyat kincid asti dhananjaya mayi sarvam idam protam sutre mani-gana iva
translation: O conqueror of wealth, there is no truth superior to Me. Everything rests upon Me, as pearls are strung on a thread.
keywords: supreme, truth, divine, pearls, thread, God

text: raso 'ham apsu kaunteya prabhasmi sasi-suryayoh pranavah sarva-vedesu sabdah khe paurusam nrsu
translation: O son of Kunti, I am the taste of water, the light of the sun and the moon, the syllable Om in the Vedic mantras; I am the sound in ether and ability in man.
keywords: divine, manifestation, Om, water, sun, moon, Vedas

text: bahunam janmanam ante jnanavan mam prapadyate vasudevah sarvam iti sa mahatma su-durlabhah
translation: After many births and deaths, he who is actually in knowledge surrenders unto Me, knowing Me to be the cause of all causes and all that is. Such a great soul is very rare.
keywords: many births, surrender, knowledge, great soul, rare, Vasudeva, cause

text: anta-kale ca mam eva smaran muktva kalevaram yah prayati sa mad-bhavam yati nasty atra samsayah
translation: And whoever, at the end of his life, quits his body remembering Me alone at once attains My nature. Of this there is no doubt.
keywords: death, remembrance, liberation, God, salvation

text: yam yam vapi smaran bhavam tyajaty ante kalevaram tam tam evaiti kaunteya sada tad-bhava-bhavitah
translation: Whatever state of being one remembers when he quits his body, O son of Kunti, that state he will attain without fail.
keywords: death, remembrance, consciousness, rebirth, final thought

text: tasmat sarvesu kalesu mam anusmara yudhya ca mayy arpita-mano-buddhir mam evaishyasy asamsayah
translation: Therefore, Arjuna, you should always think of Me in the form of Krishna and at the same time carry out your prescribed duty of fighting. With your activities dedicated to Me and your mind and intelligence fixed on Me, you will attain Me without doubt.
keywords: remember, fight, dedicated, mind, intelligence, attain, doubt

text: ananyaas cintayanto mam ye janah paryupasate tesam nityabhiyuktanam yoga-ksemam vahamy aham
translation: To those who always worship Me with exclusive devotion, meditating on My transcendental form, I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.
keywords: devotion, worship, protection, divine care, surrender

text: patram pushpam phalam toyam yo me bhaktya prayacchati tad aham bhakty-upahrtam asnami prayatatmanah
translation: If one offers Me with love and devotion a leaf, a flower, fruit or water, I will accept it.
keywords: devotion, offering, love, simplicity, bhakti

text: yat karosi yad asnasi yaj juhosi dadasi yat yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kurusva mad-arpanam
translation: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer in sacrifice, whatever you give away, whatever austerity you practice — O son of Kunti, do that as an offering to Me.
keywords: offering, sacrifice, eat, austerity, giving, dedication, surrender

text: samo 'ham sarva-bhutesu na me dvesyo 'sti na priyah ye bhajanti tu mam bhaktya mayi te tesu capy aham
translation: I envy no one, nor am I partial to anyone. I am equal to all. But whoever renders service unto Me in devotion is a friend, is in Me, and I am also a friend to him.
keywords: equality, devotion, friendship, God, impartial

text: tesam satata-yuktanam bhajatam priti-purvakam dadami buddhi-yogam tam yena mam upayanti te
translation: To those who are constantly devoted and who worship Me with love, I give the understanding by which they can come to Me.
keywords: devotion, love, worship, understanding, come to Me, buddhi yoga

text: aham atma gudakesa sarva-bhutasaya-sthitah aham adis ca madhyam ca bhutanam anta eva ca
translation: I am the Self, O Gudakesha, seated in the hearts of all creatures. I am the beginning, the middle and the end of all beings.
keywords: self, divine, beginning, end, heart, supreme

text: sanjaya uvaca evam uktva tato rajan maha-yogesvaro harih darsayam asa parthaya paramam rupam aisvaram
translation: Sanjaya said: O King, having spoken thus, the Supreme Lord of all mystic power, the Personality of Godhead, displayed His universal form to Arjuna.
keywords: Sanjaya, universal form, mystic power, Vishwarupa, Arjuna, display

text: kalo 'smi loka-ksaya-krt pravrddho lokan samahartum iha pravrttah rte 'pi tvam na bhavisyanti sarve ye 'vasthitah pratyanikesu yodhah
translation: I am Time, the great destroyer of the worlds, and I have come here to destroy all people. With the exception of you, all the soldiers here on both sides will be slain.
keywords: time, death, destroyer, cosmic form, Vishwarupa

text: tasmat tvam uttistha yaso labhasva jitva satrun bhunksva rajyam samrddham mayaivaite nihatah purvam eva nimitta-matram bhava savya-sacin
translation: Therefore get up and prepare to fight. After conquering your enemies you will enjoy a flourishing kingdom. They are already put to death by My arrangement, and you, O Savyasachi, can be but an instrument in the fight.
keywords: instrument, divine will, destiny, fight, victory

text: mayy eva mana adhatsva mayi buddhim nivesaya nivasishyasi mayy eva ata urdhvam na samsayah
translation: Fix your mind on Me alone, let your intellect dwell in Me. You shall live in Me alone thereafter. Of this there is no doubt.
keywords: mind, intellect, dwell, live, doubt, fixation, devotion

text: advesta sarva-bhutanam maitrah karuna eva ca nirmamo nirahankarah sama-duhkha-sukhah ksami
translation: One who is not envious but is a kind friend to all living entities, who does not think himself a proprietor, who is free from false ego, equal in both happiness and distress, and always forgiving.
keywords: compassion, kindness, ego-free, forgiveness, equanimity, devotion

text: arjuna uvaca prakrtim purusam caiva ksetram ksetra-jnam eva ca etad veditum icchami jnanam jneyam ca kesava
translation: Arjuna said: O Keshava, I wish to know about nature and the enjoyer, the field and the knower of the field, and knowledge and the object of knowledge.
keywords: nature, prakriti, purusha, field, knower, knowledge, Arjuna inquiry

text: samam sarvesu bhutesu tisthantam paramesvaram vinasyatsv avinasyantam yah pasyati sa pasyati
translation: One who sees the Supersoul equally present everywhere, in every living being, does not degrade himself by his mind. Thus he approaches the transcendental destination.
keywords: supersoul, equality, vision, transcendence, divine presence

text: sattvam rajas tama iti gunah prakrti-sambhavah nibadhnanti maha-baho dehe dehinam avyayam
translation: Material nature consists of three modes: goodness, passion and ignorance. When the eternal living entity comes in contact with nature, O mighty-armed Arjuna, he becomes conditioned by these modes.
keywords: gunas, sattva, rajas, tamas, nature, modes, conditioning

text: sri-bhagavan uvaca urdhva-mulam adhah-sakham asvattham prahur avyayam chandamsi yasya parnani yas tam veda sa veda-vit
translation: The Supreme Lord said: It is said that there is an imperishable banyan tree that has its roots upward and its branches down, and whose leaves are the Vedic hymns. One who knows this tree is the knower of the Vedas.
keywords: banyan tree, roots upward, Vedas, imperishable, hymns, knowledge

text: mamaivamso jiva-loke jiva-bhutah sanatanah manah-sasthanindriyani prakrti-sthani karsati
translation: The living entities in this conditioned world are My eternal fragmental parts. Due to conditioned life, they are struggling very hard with the six senses, which include the mind.
keywords: soul, fragment, divine, senses, struggle, eternal

text: sarvasya caham hrdi sannivisto mattah smrtir jnanam apohanam ca vedais ca sarvair aham eva vedyo vedanta-krd veda-vid eva caham
translation: I am seated in everyone's heart, and from Me come remembrance, knowledge and forgetfulness. By all the Vedas, I am to be known. Indeed, I am the compiler of Vedanta, and I am the knower of the Vedas.
keywords: heart, knowledge, remembrance, Vedas, Vedanta, omnipresence

text: sri-bhagavan uvaca abhayam sattva-samsuddhir jnana-yoga-vyavasthitih danam damas ca yajnas ca svadhyayas tapa arjavam
translation: The Supreme Lord said: Fearlessness, purification of one's existence, cultivation of spiritual knowledge, charity, self-control, performance of sacrifice, study of the Vedas, austerity and simplicity.
keywords: divine qualities, fearlessness, charity, self-control, simplicity

text: sattvanurupa sarvasya sraddha bhavati bharata sraddha-mayo yam purusho yo yac-chraddhah sa eva sah
translation: According to one's existence under the various modes of nature, one evolves a particular kind of faith. The living being is said to be of a particular faith according to the modes he has acquired.
keywords: faith, modes, nature, sattva, rajas, tamas, sraddha

text: datavyam iti yad danam diyate 'nupakarine dese kale ca patre ca tad danam sattvikam smrtam
translation: Charity given out of duty, without expectation of return, at the proper time and place, and to a worthy person is considered to be in the mode of goodness.
keywords: charity, duty, goodness, sattva, giving

text: etany api tu karmani sangam tyaktva phalani ca kartavyaniti me partha niscitam matam uttamam
translation: All these activities should be performed without attachment or any expectation of result. They should be performed as a matter of duty, O son of Pritha. That is My definite and supreme opinion.
keywords: duty, detachment, no expectation, supreme opinion, action, result

text: sarva-bhutesu yenaikam bhavam avyayam iksate avibhaktam vibhaktesu taj jnanam viddhi sattvikam
translation: That knowledge by which one undivided spiritual nature is seen in all living entities, though they are divided into innumerable forms, you should understand to be in the mode of goodness.
keywords: knowledge, unity, spiritual, goodness, oneness

text: yatah pravrttir bhutanam yena sarvam idam tatam sva-karmana tam abhyarcya siddhim vindati manavah
translation: By worship of the Lord, who is the source of all beings and who is all-pervading, a man can attain perfection through performing his own work.
keywords: worship, perfection, duty, devotion, work

text: bhaktya mam abhijanati yavan yas casmi tattvatah tato mam tattvato jnatva visate tad-anantaram
translation: One can understand Me as I am, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, only by devotional service. And when one is in full consciousness of Me by such devotion, he can enter into the kingdom of God.
keywords: devotion, bhakti, understanding, Supreme, kingdom of God, consciousness

text: man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru mam evaishyasi satyam te pratijane priyo 'si me
translation: Always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your homage unto Me. Thus you will come to Me without fail. I promise you this because you are My very dear friend.
keywords: devotion, worship, promise, friendship, surrender, love

text: sarva-dharman parityajya mam ekam saranam vraja aham tvam sarva-papebhyo moksayisyami ma sucah
translation: Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.
keywords: surrender, liberation, moksha, fearlessness, divine grace, salvation

text: yatra yogesvarah krsno yatra partho dhanur-dharah tatra srir vijayo bhutir dhruva nitir matir mama
translation: Wherever there is Krishna, the master of all mystics, and wherever there is Arjuna, the supreme archer, there will also certainly be opulence, victory, extraordinary power, and morality.
keywords: Krishna, Arjuna, victory, opulence, morality, conclusion

text: tapah svaadhyaaya niratam tapasvii vaagvidaam varam naradam paripapraccha vaalmiikir muni pungavam
translation: The ascetic Valmiki, engaged in penance and study of the Vedas, questioned the great sage Narada, the foremost among the eloquent sages.
keywords: Valmiki, Narada, sage, Vedas, beginning

text: ko nv asmin saampratam loke gunavaan kash ca viiryavaan dharmajna ca krtajna ca satya vaakyo drdha vratah

translation: O eternal one, who is the power behind creation, preservation and destruction, who is the substratum of the gunas, O Narayani, salutations to you.

text: sarvaabaadhaaprashamanam trailokya akhileshvari evam eva tvayaa kaaryamasmaad vairi vinaashanam

text: sarva svarupe sarveshe sarva shakti samanvite bhayebhyah traahi no devi durge devi namostute
translation: O Goddess of all forms, ruler of all, endowed with all powers — protect us from all fears. O Goddess Durga, salutations to You.

translation: Thus the great Goddess Mahamaya, the supreme Devi, spoke: I shall manifest again and again for the protection of the world.

text: ya etat paramam devyaash charitam paThate sadaa tasya sarvam bhayam naasti na ca aapadah kaascanaa
translation: Whoever always reads this supreme story of the Goddess will have no fear and no calamity.

text: praNataanam prasiidam tvam devi vishva aartihaarinii trailokya vaasinaam iDye lokaanam varadaa bhava
translation: O Goddess, be pleased with those who bow to you. O remover of the afflictions of the universe, worshipped by the inhabitants of the three worlds, be the bestower of boons.

translation: By performing karma in this world, one should wish to live a hundred years. Thus, and in no other way, can man be free from the taint of karma.

translation: The Self is one, unmoving, yet swifter than the mind. The senses cannot reach It, for It runs ahead of them. Standing still, It overtakes those who run. In It the cosmic prana sustains all activity.
keywords: Self, unmoving, swifter, mind, senses, prana, cosmic

transliterations.ml: അനേജദേകം മനസോ ജവീയോ നൈനദ് ദേവാ ആപ്നുവൻ പൂർവമർഷത്
transliterations.hi: अनेजदेकं मनसो जवीयो नैनद्देवा आप्नुवन् पूर्वमर्षत्
transliterations.ta: அநேஜதேகம் மநஸோ ஜவீயோ நைநத்தேவா ஆப்நுவந் பூர்வமர்ஷத்
transliterations.te: అనేజదేకం మనసో జవీయో నైనద్దేవా ఆప్నువన్ పూర్వమర్షత్
transliterations.kn: ಅನೇಜದೇಕಂ ಮನಸೋ ಜವೀಯೋ ನೈನದ್ದೇವಾ ಆಪ್ನುವನ್ ಪೂರ್ವಮರ್ಷತ್

text: tad ejati tan naijati tad dure tad vantike tad antarasya sarvasya tad u sarvasyaasya baahyatah
translation: It moves and It moves not. It is far and It is near. It is within all this, and It is outside all this.
keywords: moves, far, near, within, outside, paradox, Brahman

text: yas tu sarvani bhutany atmany evanupasyati sarva-bhutesu catmanam tato na vijugupsate
translation: He who sees all beings in his own Self, and his own Self in all beings, loses all fear and hatred.
keywords: self, all beings, unity, fear, hatred, oneness

text: yasmin sarvaani bhutaani aatmaivabhud vijaanataah tatra ko mohah kah shokah ekatvamanupashyataah
translation: In whom all beings have become the Self of the knower — what delusion, what sorrow can there be for the one who sees oneness?
keywords: Self, beings, knower, delusion, sorrow, oneness, unity

text: keneshitam patati preshitam manah kena pranah prathamah praiti yuktah keneshitam vacam imam vadanti cakshuh shrotram ka u devo yunakti
translation: By whom directed does the mind go towards its objects? Commanded by whom does the life-force proceed? At whose will do men utter speech? What power directs the eye and the ear?
keywords: mind, life-force, speech, senses, inquiry, Brahman

text: shrotrasya shrotram manaso mano yad vaco ha vacam sa u pranasya pranah caksusas caksur atimucya dhirah pretya asmal lokat amrta bhavanti
translation: It is the Ear of the ear, the Mind of the mind, the Speech of speech, the Life of life, the Eye of the eye. Having detached the Self from the senses, the wise become immortal.
keywords: ear, mind, speech, senses, immortal, Brahman, detachment

text: na tatra caksur gacchati na vag gacchati no manah na vidmo na vijanimo yathaitad anusisyat
translation: The eye does not reach there, nor speech, nor the mind. We do not know It, we do not understand how It can be taught.
keywords: eye, speech, mind, unknowable, teaching, Brahman, beyond

text: uttishthata jagrata prapya varan nibodhata ksurasya dhara nisita duratyaya durgam pathas tat kavayo vadanti
translation: Arise! Awake! Approach the great masters and learn. The path is sharp as a razor's edge, difficult to traverse — the wise say.
keywords: arise, awake, wisdom, razor, path, difficult, master, Nachiketa

text: atmanam rathinam viddhi shariram ratham eva tu buddhim tu sarathim viddhi manah pragraham eva ca
translation: Know the Self as the lord of the chariot, the body as the chariot. Know the intellect as the charioteer, and the mind as the reins.
keywords: self, chariot, body, intellect, mind, metaphor, Yama, soul

text: na jayate mriyate va vipascin nayam kutascin na babhuva kascit ajo nityah sasvato yam purano na hanyate hanyamane sarire
translation: The wise Self is not born, nor does it die. It did not spring from anything, nor did anything spring from it. Unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient — it is not slain when the body is slain.
keywords: self, eternal, unborn, death, immortal, soul, ancient

text: indriyebhyah para hy artha arthebhyas ca param manah manasas tu para buddhir buddher atma mahan parah
translation: Beyond the senses are the objects of sense. Beyond the objects is the mind. Beyond the mind is the intellect. Beyond the intellect is the Great Self.
keywords: senses, objects, mind, intellect, Great Self, hierarchy, consciousness

transliterations.hi: इन्द्रियेभ्यः परा ह्यर्था अर्थेभ्यश्च परं मनः
transliterations.ta: இந்த்ரியேப்யஃ பரா ஹ்யர்தா அர்தேப்யஶ்ச பரம் மநஃ

text: yada sarve pramuchyante kama ye sya hrdi shritah atha martyah amrto bhavati atra brahma samashnute
translation: When all the desires that dwell in the heart are released, then the mortal becomes immortal. Then one attains Brahman here in this very life.
keywords: desires, heart, released, immortal, Brahman, this life

text: angusthamatrah purusho madhya atmani tishthati ishanah bhuta bhavyasya na tato vijugupsate
translation: The Purusha, of the size of a thumb, resides in the center of the body. He is the Lord of the past and the future. Having known Him, one ceases to fear.
keywords: Purusha, thumb, center, Lord, past, future, fear, ceases

text: dve vidye veditavye iti ha sma yad brahmavido vadanti para caivapara ca
translation: Two kinds of knowledge must be known — this is what the knowers of Brahman say — the higher and the lower.
keywords: knowledge, higher, lower, Brahman, wisdom, vidya

text: satyameva jayate nanrtam satyena pantha vitato devayanah yenakramanty rsayo hy aptakama yatra tat satyasya paramam nidhanam
translation: Truth alone triumphs, not falsehood. Through truth the divine path is spread out by which the sages whose desires have been completely fulfilled reach where that supreme treasure of Truth resides.
keywords: truth, triumphs, divine path, sages, supreme treasure, satya

text: nayam atma pravacanena labhyo na medhaya na bahuna srutena yam evaisha vrnute tena labhyas tasyaisha atma vivrnute tanum svam
translation: This Self cannot be attained through study, nor through intellect, nor through much learning. He whom the Self chooses, by him alone is the Self attained. To him the Self reveals Its own nature.
keywords: Self, attained, chosen, study, intellect, learning, revealed, grace

text: dva suparnaa sayujaa sakhaayaa samaanam vrksham parishasvajaate tayoh anyah pippalam svaadu atti anashnan anyo abhicaakashiiti
translation: Two birds, companions and friends, sit on the same tree. One eats the sweet fruit, the other watches without eating.
keywords: two birds, tree, companions, eating, watching, soul, witness, parable

transliterations.hi: द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते

text: om ity etad aksharam idam sarvam tasyopavyakhyanam bhutam bhavad bhavishyad iti sarvam aumkara eva
translation: Om — this syllable is all this. All that is past, present, and future is truly Om. And whatever transcends the three periods of time, that too is truly Om.
keywords: Om, syllable, past, present, future, Aum, Brahman, transcendence

text: tat tvam asi shvetaketo
translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu. You are that ultimate reality, that Brahman.
keywords: tat tvam asi, you are that, Brahman, identity, self-realization, Shvetaketu, mahavakya

text: sarvam khalvidam brahma tajjalan iti santa upasita
translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From Brahman everything is born, into Brahman everything dissolves, and by Brahman everything is sustained.
keywords: Brahman, everything, creation, dissolution, sustenance, universe

text: sa ya eso nimaitad amimam sarvam tat satyam sa atma tat tvam asi shvetaketo
translation: That which is the subtlest essence — this whole world has That as its soul. That is Reality. That is the Self. That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
keywords: tat tvam asi, essence, soul, Reality, Self, Shvetaketu, mahavakya

transliterations.hi: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत् सत्यम् स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो
transliterations.ta: ஸ ய ஏஷோணிமைததாத்ம்யமிதம் ஸர்வம் தத் ஸத்யம் ஸ ஆத்மா தத்த்வமஸி ஶ்வேதகேதோ
transliterations.te: స య ఏషోణిమైతదాత్మ్యమిదం సర్వం తత్ సత్యమ్ స ఆత్మా తత్త్వమసి శ్వేతకేతో

text: sa eshaam vedaanaam rasam samavedaanaam saama chandogya upanishadaam chandogya
translation: He is the essence of the Vedas, the Sama of the Sama Vedas, the Chandogya of the Chandogya Upanishads.
keywords: essence, Vedas, Sama, Chandogya, Upanishads

text: asato ma sadgamaya tamaso ma jyotirgamaya mrtyorma amrtam gamaya
translation: Lead me from the unreal to the real, lead me from darkness to light, lead me from death to immortality.
keywords: lead me, unreal, real, darkness, light, death, immortality, prayer, Pavamana Mantra

text: aham brahmasmi
translation: I am Brahman. The individual self and the universal Self are one.
keywords: aham brahmasmi, I am Brahman, mahavakya, self-realization, identity, oneness

text: na va are patyuh kamaya patih priyo bhavaty atmanas tu kamaya patih priyo bhavati
translation: It is not for the sake of the husband that the husband is dear, but for the sake of the Self. It is not for the sake of anything else, but for the sake of the Self that everything is dear.
keywords: self, love, dear, Yajnavalkya, Maitreyi, atman

text: yatra tv asya sarvam atmaivabhud tat kena kam pashyet tat kena kam vijaniyat
translation: When to the knower of Brahman everything has become the Self, what should one see and through what? What should one know and through what?
keywords: Brahman, Self, everything, knowing, seeing, unity, oneness

text: purnam adah purnam idam purnat purnam udachyate purnasya purnam adaya purnam evavashishyate
translation: That is whole. This is whole. From the whole, the whole arises. Taking the whole from the whole, the whole alone remains.
keywords: whole, completeness, Purna, infinite, remains, Shanti mantra

transliterations.ml: പൂർണ്ണമദഃ പൂർണ്ണമിദം പൂർണ്ണാത് പൂർണ്ണമുദച്യതേ

text: sa yo ha vai tat paramam brahma veda brahmaiva bhavati naasyaabrahmavit kule bhavati
translation: He who knows that Supreme Brahman becomes Brahman indeed. In his family, no one ignorant of Brahman is born.
keywords: Brahman, knows, becomes, family, ignorant, Supreme

text: satyam vada dharmam cara svadhyayan ma pramadah acarya priyam dhanam ahrtya prajatantum ma vyavacchetsih
translation: Speak the truth. Practice dharma. Do not neglect study. Having brought the teacher's fee, do not cut off the line of progeny.
keywords: truth, dharma, study, teacher, duty, convocation

text: anando brahmeti vyajanat anandadhyeva khalvimani bhutani jayante anandena jatani jivanti anandam prayanty abhisamvisanti
translation: He knew that Brahman is bliss. From bliss all beings are born, by bliss they are sustained, and into bliss they return and merge.
keywords: bliss, Brahman, ananda, born, sustained, merge, joy

text: matrdevo bhava pitrdevo bhava acharyadevo bhava atithidevo bhava
translation: Let your mother be a god to you. Let your father be a god to you. Let your teacher be a god to you. Let your guest be a god to you.
keywords: mother, father, teacher, guest, god, respect, duty, reverence

transliterations.hi: मातृदेवो भव पितृदेवो भव आचार्यदेवो भव अतिथिदेवो भव
transliterations.ta: மாத்ருதேவோ பவ பித்ருதேவோ பவ ஆசார்யதேவோ பவ அதிதிதேவோ பவ
transliterations.te: మాతృదేవో భవ పితృదేవో భవ ఆచార్యదేవో భవ అతిథిదేవో భవ
transliterations.kn: ಮಾತೃದೇವೋ ಭವ ಪಿತೃದೇವೋ ಭವ ಆಚಾರ್ಯದೇವೋ ಭವ ಅತಿಥಿದೇವೋ ಭವ

text: atha yoganushasanam
translation: Now begins the exposition of yoga.
keywords: yoga, beginning, discipline, exposition

text: yogash chitta vrtti nirodhah
translation: Yoga is the cessation of the fluctuations of the mind.
keywords: yoga, mind, cessation, fluctuations, chitta, vritti, definition

text: tada drashtuh svarupe avasthanam
translation: Then the Seer abides in its own true nature.
keywords: seer, true nature, self, witness, consciousness

text: vrtti sarupyam itaratra
translation: At other times, the Seer identifies with the fluctuations of the mind.
keywords: Seer, identifies, mind, fluctuations, other times

text: abhyasa vairagyabhyam tannirodhah
translation: The cessation of mental fluctuations is brought about by persistent practice and non-attachment.
keywords: practice, non-attachment, vairagya, abhyasa, cessation, discipline

text: sa tu dirgha kala nairantarya satkara asevito drdha bhumih
translation: Practice becomes firmly grounded when it is cultivated for a long time, without interruption, and with devotion.
keywords: practice, long time, uninterrupted, devotion, grounded, discipline

text: heyam duhkham anagatam
translation: The suffering which has not yet come can and should be avoided.
keywords: suffering, future, avoid, prevention, wisdom

text: ishvara pranidhanad va
translation: Or by devotion to the Supreme Lord.
keywords: devotion, Supreme Lord, Ishvara, surrender, alternative

text: vyadhi styana samshaya pramada alasya avirati bhrantidarshana alabdha bhumikatva anavasthitatva chitta vikshepa te antarayah
translation: Disease, dullness, doubt, carelessness, laziness, sensuality, false perception, failure to reach firm ground, and instability — these distractions of the mind are the obstacles.
keywords: obstacles, disease, doubt, laziness, distractions, mind

text: maitri karuna mudita upekshanam sukha duhkha punya apunya vishayanam bhavanatah chitta prasadanam
translation: By cultivating attitudes of friendliness toward the happy, compassion for the unhappy, delight in the virtuous, and equanimity toward the non-virtuous, the mind retains its undisturbed calmness.
keywords: friendliness, compassion, delight, equanimity, calmness, mind, virtue

text: tapah svadhyaya ishvarapranidhanani kriya yogah
translation: Austerity, self-study, and surrender to God constitute the yoga of action (Kriya Yoga).
keywords: austerity, self-study, surrender, God, kriya yoga, tapas

text: avidya asmita raga dvesha abhiniveshaha pancha kleshah
translation: Ignorance, egoism, attachment, aversion, and clinging to life are the five afflictions.
keywords: ignorance, ego, attachment, aversion, fear, klesha, afflictions

text: yama niyama asana pranayama pratyahara dharana dhyana samadhayo ashtau angani
translation: The eight limbs of yoga are: restraints, observances, posture, breath control, sense withdrawal, concentration, meditation, and absorption.
keywords: eight limbs, ashtanga, yama, niyama, asana, pranayama, meditation, samadhi

text: ahimsa satya asteya brahmacharya aparigraha yamah
translation: Non-violence, truthfulness, non-stealing, celibacy, and non-possessiveness are the five restraints (yamas).
keywords: ahimsa, satya, non-violence, truth, yama, restraints, ethics

text: vitarka badhane pratipaksha bhavanam
translation: When disturbed by negative thoughts, cultivate the opposite.
keywords: negative, thoughts, opposite, cultivation, pratipaksha bhavana

text: ahimsa pratishthayam tat sannidhau vairatyagah
translation: When non-violence is firmly established, hostility ceases in the presence of such a one.
keywords: non-violence, ahimsa, hostility, ceases, presence, firmly

text: satya pratishthayam kriya phalashrayatvam
translation: When truthfulness is firmly established, actions and their results become subservient to the truthful one.
keywords: truth, satya, actions, results, established, power

text: santoshad anuttamah sukha labhah
translation: From contentment, supreme happiness is gained.
keywords: contentment, happiness, supreme, santosha, joy

text: sthira sukham asanam
translation: Posture should be steady and comfortable.
keywords: posture, steady, comfortable, asana, stability

text: tato dvandva anabhighatah
translation: From mastery of posture, one is no longer affected by the pairs of opposites.
keywords: posture, mastery, opposites, unaffected, duality, asana

text: desha bandhah chittasya dharana
translation: Concentration is fixing the mind on a single point or region.
keywords: concentration, mind, focus, dharana, single point

text: tatra pratyaya ekatanata dhyanam
translation: Meditation is the continuous flow of awareness toward that point.
keywords: meditation, continuous, flow, awareness, dhyana

text: tad eva artha matra nirbhasam svarupa shunyam iva samadhih
translation: When the object of meditation alone shines forth, as if devoid of one's own form, that is samadhi.
keywords: meditation, shines, samadhi, form, devoid, object

text: purusha artha shunyanam gunanam pratiprasavah kaivalyam svarupa pratishtha va chiti shaktir iti
translation: Liberation (Kaivalya) is the state when the gunas cease serving the purpose of the Self. The power of pure consciousness becomes established in its own nature.
keywords: liberation, kaivalya, gunas, consciousness, freedom, self

text: ahimsa paramo dharmah dharma himsa tathaiva cha
translation: Non-violence is the highest dharma, and so too is all justified violence in service of dharma.
keywords: ahimsa, non-violence, dharma, violence, highest law

text: na hi satyat paro dharmah
translation: There is no dharma higher than truth.
keywords: truth, dharma, highest, satya

text: dharma eva hato hanti dharmo rakshati rakshitah tasmat dharmo na hantavyah ma no dharmo hato vadhit
translation: Dharma destroys those who destroy it. Dharma protects those who protect it. Therefore dharma should not be destroyed, lest destroyed dharma destroy us.
keywords: dharma, protection, destruction, law, righteousness

text: yatha sarvasya bhuutasya pitaa maata ca madhavah tatha tisthema bhutanam hitam carantah
translation: As Madhava is the father and mother of all beings, so should we conduct ourselves for the welfare of all creatures.
keywords: welfare, all beings, compassion, duty, Madhava

text: shreyah svadharmo vigunah paradharmat svanushthitat sva dharme nidhanam shreyah paradharmo bhayavahah
translation: Better is one's own dharma, though imperfect, than the dharma of another well discharged. Better is death in one's own dharma; the dharma of another brings danger.
keywords: svadharma, own duty, another's duty, danger, death, better

text: na jatu kamah kamanam upabhogena shaamyati havisha krsnavartmeva bhuya evabhivardhate
translation: Desire never ends by enjoying the objects of desire. Like fire fed with ghee, it only increases more and more.
keywords: desire, fire, ghee, increase, never ends, enjoyment

text: parasparam bhavayanntah shreyah param avapsyatha
translation: By mutual cooperation and support, you shall attain the highest good.
keywords: cooperation, mutual, support, highest good, welfare

text: udyamena hi sidhyanti kaaryaani na manorathaih na hi suptasya simhasya pravishanti mukhe mrgaah
translation: Tasks are accomplished by effort, not by wishes. Prey does not walk into the mouth of a sleeping lion.
keywords: effort, wishes, lion, sleeping, tasks, accomplished

transliterations.hi: उद्यमेन हि सिध्यन्ति कार्याणि न मनोरथैः

text: vidura uvaca atmanam prthivim patnim putran bandhums tathaa suhrt shad etan samyag avekseta na pramatted dhanaanvitah
translation: Vidura said: A wise man should carefully attend to six things: his own self, his country, his wife, his children, his relatives, and his friends. He should never be negligent about wealth.
keywords: wisdom, Vidura, six things, self, family, wealth, negligence

text: panca panchabhir utthaya bhutva sthira shayanam cha shama dama ahimsa satya arjavam dharma lakshanam
translation: Tranquility, self-control, non-violence, truthfulness, and simplicity — these are the marks of dharma.
keywords: tranquility, self-control, non-violence, truth, simplicity, dharma, marks

text: krodhah pranashanam dattam mano bhedo ripo jayah aashvasah sarva satveshu daanam ishtam dvija archana
translation: Anger leads to ruin, a composed mind conquers enemies. Compassion to all beings, generosity, and reverence for the learned — these are the highest virtues.
keywords: anger, ruin, mind, enemies, compassion, generosity, virtues

text: budho na pashyed ripum apramattah ripum pramattas tu ripur na pashyet
translation: A wise man is always watchful of enemies, while a careless man fails to notice even an obvious enemy.
keywords: wisdom, watchful, enemies, careless, vigilance, Vidura

text: satyena dharyate prthivi satyena tapate ravih satyena vaayavah vaanti sarvam satye pratishthitam
translation: By truth the earth is sustained, by truth the sun shines, by truth the winds blow — everything is established in truth.
keywords: truth, earth, sun, winds, established, sustained

text: aalasyam hi manushyaanaam sharirastho mahaan ripuh naasty udyamasamo bandhuh kurvaano naavasiidati
translation: Laziness is the greatest enemy dwelling in the human body. There is no friend equal to effort — one who strives never perishes.
keywords: laziness, enemy, effort, friend, striving, never perishes

transliterations.hi: आलस्यं हि मनुष्याणां शरीरस्थो महान् रिपुः

text: sarve bhavantu sukhinah sarve santu niramayah sarve bhadrani pashyantu ma kashcit duhkha bhag bhavet
translation: May all be happy, may all be free from illness. May all see what is auspicious, may no one suffer.
keywords: happiness, health, auspicious, suffering, universal prayer, blessing

text: shruuyataam dharma sarvasvam shrutvaa caiva anuvartate aatmanah pratikuulaani pareshaam na samacharet
translation: Listen to the essence of dharma and having listened, follow it: Do not do unto others what you would not have them do unto you.
keywords: golden rule, dharma, essence, others, reciprocity, ethics

text: yatha hi pankena pankam saadhayiShyasi nahi saa shakyaa yaa kriyaa kartum
translation: One cannot wash away mud with mud. Wrong cannot be righted with more wrong. Only righteousness can overcome unrighteousness.
keywords: mud, wrong, right, righteousness, method, justice

text: daanena tulyam na bhavet tapah na vidyaa na balam na parakramah daane prasanna bhavanti devataa daanena sarvam vashamaapayet
translation: There is no penance equal to charity, no knowledge, no strength, no valor. The gods are pleased by charity. By charity one can bring everything under control.
keywords: charity, penance, gods, pleased, control, strength

text: sukhasya muulam dharmo dharmasyaiva krpa mulam krpayaash cha gurum sevaa tato daivam prasidati
translation: The root of happiness is dharma, the root of dharma is compassion, the root of compassion is service to the guru; thereby the divine is pleased.
keywords: happiness, dharma, compassion, guru, service, divine, root

text: vinasho naasti daivena na cha bhagyena cha kvachit karmanaam phala samyogaat praarabdham phalam aapnuyaat
translation: Destruction comes not by fate alone, nor by destiny. Through the connection of one's own actions, one obtains the results of what was begun.
keywords: fate, destiny, actions, results, karma, self-determination

text: naasti vidyaa samam chakshur naasti satya samam tapah naasti raaga samam dukham naasti tyaaga samam sukham
translation: There is no eye like knowledge, no penance like truth, no sorrow like attachment, and no happiness like renunciation.
keywords: knowledge, truth, attachment, renunciation, eye, sorrow, happiness, penance

text: na jaatu kaamaan na bhayaan na lobhaat dharmam tyajet jeevitasyaapi hetoh dharmah nityah sukha duhkhe tu anityee jeevo nityah deha tu asya anityah
translation: One should never abandon dharma out of desire, fear, greed, or even for the sake of life itself. Dharma is eternal; pleasure and pain are transient. The soul is eternal; the body is transient.
keywords: dharma, eternal, desire, fear, greed, soul, body, transient

transliterations.hi: न जातु कामान्न भयान्न लोभाद्धर्मं त्यजेज्जीवितस्यापि हेतोः

text: yatra yogeshvarah krishno yatra partho dhanurdharah tatra shrir vijayo bhutir dhruva nitir matir mama
translation: Where there is Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, and where there is Arjuna, the wielder of the bow — there will be fortune, victory, prosperity, and firm righteousness.
keywords: Krishna, Arjuna, victory, fortune, prosperity, righteousness

text: kale kale hi dharmasya pravrttih cha pravartate kalah sarvesham bhutanam pravrttim cha nivarttanam
translation: From age to age, dharma manifests and evolves. Time governs the rise and fall of all beings.
keywords: time, dharma, age, evolution, rise, fall, beings

text: vinashyatsv api raajyeshu dhruvo jayati dharmavaan adharmo naahi shaashvatah dharmah shaashvatah smritah
translation: Even when kingdoms are destroyed, the righteous person ultimately triumphs. Adharma is never eternal; only dharma is eternal.
keywords: dharma, eternal, kingdom, righteous, triumph, adharma

text: agnim ile purohitam yajnasya devam ritvijam hotaram ratnadhatamam
translation: I praise Agni, the chosen Priest, God, minister of sacrifice, the hotar, lavishest of wealth.
keywords: Agni, fire, praise, sacrifice, priest, wealth, Rig Veda, first hymn

text: ekam sad vipra bahudha vadanty agnim yamam matarishvanam ahuh
translation: Truth is One; the wise call it by many names — they call it Agni, Yama, Matarishvan.
keywords: truth, one, many names, Agni, Yama, unity, diversity, ekam sat

text: nasadiya sukta: nasad asin no sad asit tadanim nasid rajo no vyoma paro yat
translation: Then there was neither existence nor non-existence; there was no atmosphere, no sky beyond. What covered it? Where was it? In whose protection?
keywords: creation, hymn, existence, non-existence, Nasadiya Sukta, origin, cosmos

text: om purnamadah purnamidam purnat purnamudachyate purnasya purnamadaya purnamevavashishyate
translation: That is whole, this is whole. From the whole, the whole arises. Taking the whole from the whole, only the whole remains.
keywords: whole, completeness, infinite, purna, Brahman, fullness, Shanti mantra

text: gayatri mantra: tat savitur varenyam bhargo devasya dhimahi dhiyo yo nah prachodayat
translation: We meditate on the divine light of the radiant Sun. May it inspire our understanding and illuminate our intellect.
keywords: Gayatri mantra, Sun, meditation, light, intellect, prayer, Savitri

text: indram mitra varunam agnim ahur atho divyah sa suparno garutman ekam sad vipra bahudha vadanty
translation: They call It Indra, Mitra, Varuna, Agni, and the divine bird Garutman. The sages speak of the One Reality by many names.
keywords: Indra, Mitra, Varuna, Agni, One Reality, many names, unity, ekam sat

transliterations.hi: इन्द्रं मित्रं वरुणमग्निमाहुरथो दिव्यः स सुपर्णो गरुत्मान्
transliterations.ta: இந்த்ரம் மித்ரம் வருணமக்நிமாஹுரதோ திவ்யஃ ஸ ஸுபர்ணோ கருத்மான்

text: asatyenprthivi tatatha dyam satyenottabhita ritena adityastishthanti divi somo adhi shritah
translation: By Truth the earth is sustained, by Truth the heaven is held up, by Truth the sun and stars stand in the sky.
keywords: truth, earth, heaven, sun, stars, sustained, Rita, cosmic order

text: aa no bhadrah kratavo yantu vishvatah
translation: Let noble thoughts come to us from every direction.
keywords: noble, thoughts, directions, universal, prayer, openness

transliterations.hi: आ नो भद्राः क्रतवो यन्तु विश्वतः
transliterations.ta: ஆ நோ பத்ராஃ க்ரதவோ யந்து விஶ்வதஃ
transliterations.te: ఆ నో భద్రాః క్రతవో యన్తు విశ్వతః
transliterations.kn: ಆ ನೋ ಭದ್ರಾಃ ಕ್ರತವೋ ಯನ್ತು ವಿಶ್ವತಃ

text: sarasvatii mahabhage vidye pushtikari stubhyam
translation: O great and fortunate Saraswati, who nourishes knowledge, I praise you.
keywords: Saraswati, knowledge, nourishes, praise, goddess

text: udayann adya mitramaha arohan uttaraam divam hrdrogham mama surya harimaanam cha nasaya
translation: O Sun, rising today as the great friend, ascending the highest heaven — destroy the disease of my heart and my sorrow.
keywords: Sun, friend, heaven, disease, heart, sorrow, healing

text: sarve bhavantu sukhinah sarve santu niramayah sarve bhadraani pashyantu ma kashchid duhkhabhag bhavet
translation: May all be happy, may all be free from disease, may all see auspiciousness, may none suffer.
keywords: happy, disease-free, auspicious, suffering, universal prayer

transliterations.hi: सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः सर्वे सन्तु निरामयाः
transliterations.ta: ஸர்வே பவந்து ஸுகிநஃ ஸர்வே ஸந்து நிராமயாஃ
transliterations.te: సర్వే భవన్తు సుఖినః సర్వే సన్తు నిరామయాః
transliterations.kn: ಸರ್ವೇ ಭವನ್ತು ಸುಖಿನಃ ಸರ್ವೇ ಸನ್ತು ನಿರಾಮಯಾಃ
transliterations.ml: സർവേ ഭവന്തു സുഖിനഃ സർവേ സന്തു നിരാമയാഃ

text: om sahana vavatu sahanau bhunaktu saha viryam karavavahai tejasvi navadhitamastu ma vidvishavahai
translation: May we be protected together, may we be nourished together. May we work together with great energy. May our studies be brilliant. May we never quarrel.
keywords: protection, nourishment, energy, study, peace, Shanti mantra, teacher, student

text: om bhadram karnebhih shrunuyaama devaa bhadram pashyema akshabhir yajatraah sthirair angais tushtuvaamsas tanuubhih vyashema devahitam yad aayuh
translation: O Gods, may we hear with our ears what is auspicious. May we see what is auspicious. May we enjoy the life allotted by the gods with strong and healthy bodies.
keywords: ears, eyes, auspicious, health, body, life, gods, blessing

text: om dyauh shantir antariksham shantih prthivi shantir apah shantir oshadhayah shantih
translation: Om. May there be peace in the heavens, peace in the atmosphere, peace on earth, peace in the waters, peace in the herbs.
keywords: peace, heavens, atmosphere, earth, waters, herbs, Shanti, Om

transliterations.hi: ॐ द्यौः शान्तिरन्तरिक्षं शान्तिः पृथिवी शान्तिरापः शान्तिरोषधयः शान्तिः
transliterations.ta: ஓம் த்யௌஃ ஶாந்திரந்தரிக்ஷம் ஶாந்திஃ ப்ருதிவீ ஶாந்திராபஃ ஶாந்திரோஷதயஃ ஶாந்திஃ
transliterations.te: ఓం ద్యౌః శాన్తిరన్తరిక్షం శాన్తిః పృథివీ శాన్తిరాపః శాన్తిరోషధయః శాన్తిః
transliterations.kn: ಓಂ ದ್ಯೌಃ ಶಾನ್ತಿರನ್ತರಿಕ್ಷಂ ಶಾನ್ತಿಃ ಪೃಥಿವೀ ಶಾನ್ತಿರಾಪಃ ಶಾನ್ತಿರೋಷಧಯಃ ಶಾನ್ತಿಃ

text: asato ma sad gamaya tamaso ma jyotir gamaya mrtyor ma amrtam gamaya
translation: Lead me from the unreal to the real, from darkness to light, from death to immortality.
keywords: unreal, real, darkness, light, death, immortality, Pavamana

transliterations.hi: असतो मा सद्गमय तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय
transliterations.ta: அஸதோ மா ஸத்கமய தமஸோ மா ஜ்யோதிர்கமய ம்ருத்யோர்மா அம்ருதம் கமய

text: ishaavaasyam idam sarvam yatkincha jagatyaam jagat
translation: All this — whatever moves in this moving world — is pervaded by the Lord.
keywords: Lord, pervaded, world, moving, everything, Isha

text: agna a yahi vitaye grnano havya dhataye ni hota satsi barhishi
translation: O Agni, come to the feast, praised one, to the offering. Sit as the priest upon the sacred grass.
keywords: Agni, fire, feast, priest, offering, sacred, Sama Veda

text: udgiitham etad bhagavato vishno stutam sarvam idam vishno mahaatmyam samprajaayate
translation: This Udgitha (sacred chant) praises Lord Vishnu. All this greatness of Vishnu is manifest everywhere.
keywords: Udgitha, chant, Vishnu, greatness, manifest, praise

text: sa eshaam vedaanaam rasah samam gaayati praanasya praanam vedaanaam vedam
translation: He who sings the Sama, sings the essence of the Vedas, the life of life, the Veda of Vedas.
keywords: Sama, essence, Vedas, life, singing, sacred

text: prthivim shantam dyo shantam shantam idam urv antariksham shantah samudroh salilam shantam nah santvoshadhayah
translation: May the earth be peaceful, may the sky be peaceful. May the vast space between be peaceful. May the waters be peaceful, and may the herbs be peaceful for us.
keywords: peace, earth, sky, waters, herbs, prayer, Shanti, nature

text: mata bhumih putro aham prthivyah parjanyah pita sa u nah pipartu
translation: Earth is my mother, I am her son. Parjanya (the rain god) is my father — may he nourish us.
keywords: earth, mother, son, rain, father, nourish, nature, Prithvi

text: vasudhaiva kutumbakam
translation: The world is one family.
keywords: world, family, unity, universal brotherhood, vasudhaiva kutumbakam

transliterations.ml: വസുധൈവ കുടുംബകം

text: ayam me hasto bhagavaan ayam me bhagavattarah ayam me vishva bheshajah ayam shivaabhimarshanah
translation: This hand of mine is blessed, this hand brings fortune. This hand is the universal medicine, this hand gives the auspicious touch.
keywords: hand, blessed, fortune, medicine, auspicious, healing

transliterations.hi: अयं मे हस्तो भगवान् अयं मे भगवत्तरः

text: bhumirmaata aakaashahpita oshadhayo me mitragano bhavantu
translation: The earth is my mother, the sky is my father, may the herbs be my friends.
keywords: earth, mother, sky, father, herbs, friends, nature

text: shri guru charan saroj raj nij manu mukuru sudhari baranaun raghubar bimal jasu jo dayaku phal chari
translation: Cleansing the mirror of my mind with the pollen-dust of the lotus feet of the Guru, I describe the untarnished glory of Rama, which bestows the four fruits of life.
keywords: guru, Rama, glory, four fruits, cleansing, mind, Tulsidas

text: buddhihina tanu jaanike sumiraun pavan kumar bal buddhi vidya dehu mohi harahu kalesa vikaar
translation: Knowing myself to be ignorant, I pray to you, O Hanuman, son of the Wind God: Grant me strength, wisdom and knowledge, and remove my afflictions and impurities.
keywords: Hanuman, wisdom, strength, knowledge, prayer, afflictions, Pavan Kumar

text: jai hanuman gyan gun sagar jai kapis tihun lok ujagar
translation: Victory to Hanuman, the ocean of wisdom and virtue. Victory to the Lord of monkeys, illuminator of the three worlds.
keywords: Hanuman, victory, wisdom, virtue, ocean, three worlds, illuminator

text: ram doot atulit bal dhama anjani putra pavan sut nama
translation: You are the messenger of Rama, the abode of matchless strength, the son of Anjani and the Wind God.
keywords: Hanuman, Rama, messenger, strength, Anjani, Wind God, Vayu

text: mahabir bikram bajrangi kumati nivar sumati ke sangi
translation: O great hero, valiant and strong as a thunderbolt, you dispel evil intellect and are the companion of those with good sense.
keywords: hero, valiant, thunderbolt, evil, intellect, companion, wisdom

text: kanchan baran biraj subesa kanan kundal kunchit kesa
translation: Your golden-hued body is resplendent, wearing beautiful garments, with earrings and curly hair.
keywords: golden, body, resplendent, beautiful, Hanuman, description

text: bhima roop dhari asur samhare ramachandra ke kaaj sanvare
translation: Assuming a fearsome form, you destroyed the demons and accomplished all the tasks of Lord Rama.
keywords: fearsome, demons, destruction, Rama, tasks, accomplished

text: sankat kate mite sab pira jo sumirai hanuman balbira
translation: All difficulties are removed and all pain ends for those who remember the mighty Hanuman.
keywords: difficulties, pain, remember, Hanuman, mighty, protection

text: pavan tanay sankat haran mangal murati roop ram laxman sita sahit hriday basahu sur bhoop
translation: O son of the Wind, remover of calamities, embodiment of auspiciousness — together with Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita, dwell forever in my heart, O king of gods.
keywords: Hanuman, Wind, calamity, auspicious, Rama, Sita, Lakshmana, heart, blessing

text: jai jai jai hanuman gosain krupa karahu gurudev ki naain
translation: Victory, victory, victory to Lord Hanuman! Bestow your grace upon us as our supreme teacher.
keywords: victory, Hanuman, grace, teacher, guru, blessing

transliterations.ml: ജയ് ജയ് ജയ് ഹനുമാൻ ഗോസാഈൻ കൃപാ കരഹു ഗുരുദേവ് കീ നായിൻ
transliterations.hi: जय जय जय हनुमान गोसाईं कृपा करहु गुरुदेव की नाईं
transliterations.ta: ஜய் ஜய் ஜய் ஹநுமான் கோஸாயீன் க்ருபா கரஹு குருதேவ் கீ நாயீன்

text: jo sat baar paath kar koi chhutahi bandi maha sukh hoi
translation: Whoever recites this a hundred times is released from bondage and attains great bliss.
keywords: recitation, hundred, bondage, released, bliss, merit

transliterations.ml: ജോ സത് ബാർ പാഠ് കർ കോയീ ഛൂടഹി ബന്ദി മഹാ സുഖ് ഹോയി
transliterations.hi: जो सत बार पाठ कर कोई छूटहि बन्दि महा सुख होई
transliterations.ta: ஜோ ஸத் பார் பாட் கர் கோயீ சூடஹி பந்தி மஹா ஸுக் ஹோயீ

text: vidyavaan guni ati chatur ram kaaj karibe ko aatur
translation: You are learned, virtuous, and extremely clever, always eager to accomplish Lord Rama's work.
keywords: learned, virtuous, clever, eager, Rama, work

text: prabhu charitra sunibe ko rasiya ram lakhan sita man basiya
translation: You delight in hearing the deeds of the Lord. Rama, Lakshmana and Sita dwell in your heart.
keywords: deeds, Lord, delight, Rama, Lakshmana, Sita, heart

text: sukshma roop dhari siyahi dikhava vikat roop dhari lanka jarava
translation: In a tiny form you appeared before Sita. In a terrible form you burnt Lanka.
keywords: tiny, Sita, terrible, Lanka, burnt, form

text: laaye sajivan lakhan jiyaaye shri raghuvir harashi ur laaye
translation: You brought the life-giving herb (Sanjeevani) and revived Lakshmana. Sri Rama embraced you with joy.
keywords: Sanjeevani, herb, Lakshmana, revived, Rama, joy, embrace

text: raghupati kinhi bahut badai tum mama priya bharat hi sam bhai
translation: Lord Rama praised you greatly, saying: 'You are dear to me as my brother Bharata.'
keywords: Rama, praised, dear, brother, Bharata, love

text: sahas badan tumharo jas gaave as kahi shripati kanth lagaave
translation: The thousand-headed serpent (Shesha) sings your glory, and Lord Vishnu embraces you.
keywords: Shesha, serpent, glory, Vishnu, embrace, singing

text: sanakaadik brahmaadi muneesa narada sarada sahit aheesa
translation: Sanaka, Brahma, sages, Narada, Saraswati, and the king of serpents — all praise you.
keywords: Sanaka, Brahma, sages, Narada, Saraswati, serpent, praise

text: yam kuber digpal jahan te kavi kovid kahi sake kahan
translation: Even Yama, Kubera, and the guardians of directions, poets and scholars — none can describe your glory.
keywords: Yama, Kubera, directions, poets, scholars, glory, indescribable

text: tum upkar sugreevahin keenha ram milaye raj pad deenha
translation: You did a great favor to Sugriva by introducing him to Rama, who then gave him the kingdom.
keywords: favor, Sugriva, Rama, kingdom, introduction, alliance

text: sarvam vishnumayam jagat
translation: The entire universe is pervaded by Vishnu.
keywords: Vishnu, universe, pervaded, all-encompassing, divine

text: janma karma ca me divyam evam yo vetti tattvatah tyaktva deham punar janma naiti mam eti so arjuna
translation: One who truly understands My divine birth and activities, upon leaving the body, is not born again but comes to Me, O Arjuna.
keywords: divine birth, liberation, rebirth, Vishnu, knowledge, moksha

text: yasya prasaadad bhagavat prasaado yasya aprasaadaan na gatih kuto api dhyaayan stuvams tasya yashas trisandhyam
translation: By whose grace one attains divine grace, without whose grace there is no progress — one should meditate on and praise his glory at the three junctions of the day.
keywords: grace, divine, guru, meditation, praise, progress

text: hari om tat sat sri narayana tu purushottamah
translation: Hari Om Tat Sat — Sri Narayana is the Supreme Person.
keywords: Hari, Om, Narayana, Supreme, Person

transliterations.hi: हरि ॐ तत् सत् श्री नारायण तु पुरुषोत्तमः

text: shanti shanti shanti om aadhi daivika aadhi bhautika aadhi aatmika taapa nivaaranam
translation: Om Peace Peace Peace — removal of sufferings arising from divine, physical, and spiritual sources.
keywords: peace, Om, sufferings, divine, physical, spiritual

text: yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata abhyutthanam adharmasya vishnu avataram tatha
translation: Whenever dharma declines and adharma rises, Lord Vishnu manifests an incarnation.
keywords: dharma, decline, Vishnu, avatar, incarnation, adharma

text: dashaavataarah purushottamasya matsya kurma varaha narasimha vamana parasurama rama krishna buddha kalki
translation: The ten incarnations of the Supreme Person: Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Krishna, Buddha, and Kalki.
keywords: ten avatars, Dashavatara, Matsya, Krishna, Kalki, Vishnu

text: om namah shivaya shantaya karanah traya hetave nivedayami chatmanam tvam gatih parameshvara
translation: Om, salutations to Shiva, the peaceful one, the cause of the three worlds. I surrender myself to you. You are the supreme refuge, O Lord.
keywords: Shiva, salutation, peaceful, three worlds, surrender, refuge, Om Namah Shivaya

text: karpur gauram karunavataram sansara saram bhujagendra haaram sada vasantam hridayaravinde bhavam bhavani sahitam namami
translation: I bow to Lord Shiva, who is white as camphor, the incarnation of compassion, the essence of the world, who wears the king of serpents as a garland, and who always dwells in the lotus of the heart along with Goddess Bhavani.
keywords: Shiva, camphor, compassion, serpent, heart, Bhavani, prayer

text: mahamrityunjaya mantra: tryambakam yajamahe sugandhim pushtivardhanam urvarukamiva bandhanan mrityor mukshiya maamritat
translation: We worship the three-eyed Lord (Shiva) who nourishes all and increases sweetness. As the cucumber is freed from its bondage to the vine, may we be liberated from death, not from immortality.
keywords: Mahamrityunjaya, Shiva, three-eyed, liberation, death, immortality, mantra, healing

transliterations.ml: ത്ര്യംബകം യജാമഹേ സുഗന്ധിം പുഷ്ടിവർദ്ധനം ഉർവ്വാരുകമിവ ബന്ധനാൻ മൃത്യോർ മുക്ഷീയ മാമൃതാത്
transliterations.hi: त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम् उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय मामृतात्
transliterations.ta: த்ர்யம்பகம் யஜாமஹே ஸுகந்திம் புஷ்டிவர்தநம் உர்வாருகமிவ பந்தநான்ம்ருத்யோர்முக்ஷீய மாம்ருதாத்
transliterations.te: త్ర్యంబకం యజామహే సుగన్ధిం పుష్టివర్ధనమ్ ఉర్వారుకమివ బన్ధనాన్మృత్యోర్ముక్షీయ మామృతాత్
transliterations.kn: ತ್ರ್ಯಂಬಕಂ ಯಜಾಮಹೇ ಸುಗಂಧಿಂ ಪುಷ್ಟಿವರ್ಧನಮ್ ಉರ್ವಾರುಕಮಿವ ಬಂಧನಾನ್ಮೃತ್ಯೋರ್ಮುಕ್ಷೀಯ ಮಾಮೃತಾತ್

text: shivaaya vishnu rupaya shiva rupaya vishnave shivasya hridayam vishnuh vishnos cha hridayam shivah
translation: Shiva is in the form of Vishnu, and Vishnu is in the form of Shiva. Vishnu is the heart of Shiva, and Shiva is the heart of Vishnu.
keywords: Shiva, Vishnu, heart, unity, form, oneness

text: lingashtakam idam punyam yah patheth shiva sannidhau shivalokam avaapnoti shivena saha modate
translation: One who recites this sacred Lingashtakam in the presence of Shiva attains Shiva's abode and rejoices with Shiva.
keywords: Lingashtakam, Shiva, abode, recitation, sacred, rejoice

text: nandi uvaca om namah shivaaya shubham shubham kuru kuru shivam
translation: Nandi said: Om Namah Shivaya — make all auspicious, make all auspicious, O Shiva.
keywords: Nandi, Om Namah Shivaya, auspicious, Shiva, prayer

text: janmady asya yatah anvayad itaratas ca arthesv abhijnah svarat tene brahma hrda ya adi kavaye
translation: I meditate upon Him from whom this universe originates, by whom it is sustained, and in whom it is dissolved — He who is the omniscient, self-luminous being who revealed the Vedas to the first poet.
keywords: Brahman, universe, origin, sustenance, dissolution, omniscient, Vedas

text: vasudevah sarvam iti sa mahatma sudurlabhah
translation: One who knows that Vasudeva (Krishna) is everything — such a great soul is very rare to find.
keywords: Vasudeva, Krishna, everything, great soul, rare, devotion

text: kaler dosha nidhe rajann asti hy eko mahan gunah kirtanad eva krishnasya mukta sangah param vrajet
translation: O King, in this age of Kali, which is an ocean of faults, there is one great quality: simply by chanting the names of Krishna, one can become free from all bondage and attain the supreme goal.
keywords: Kali Yuga, chanting, Krishna, liberation, nama, bondage, supreme goal

text: yad anudhyasinah yukta karmana anusmara tyo pi hrida purushottamam
translation: By constantly meditating with a disciplined mind upon the Supreme Being in one's heart, even while performing prescribed duties, one attains perfection.
keywords: meditation, heart, Supreme Being, duties, perfection, discipline

text: yam brahmaa varunendra rudra marutah stunvanti divyaih stavaih vedaih sanga pada kramopanishadaih gaayanti yam saamagaah
translation: He whom Brahma, Varuna, Indra, Rudra, and the Maruts praise with divine hymns; whom the Sama Veda singers celebrate through Vedic mantras and Upanishads.
keywords: Brahma, Indra, Rudra, divine hymns, Sama Veda, Upanishads, praise

transliterations.hi: यं ब्रह्मा वरुणेन्द्ररुद्रमरुतः स्तुन्वन्ति दिव्यैः स्तवैः

text: dhyaanavasthita tad gatena manasa pashyanti yam yoginah yasyaantam na viduh sura sura ganaa devaaya tasmai namah
